definitions.definition: A compound in which a central metal atom or ion is surrounded by one or more ligands, which are ions or molecules that donate a pair of electrons to form a coordinate covalent bond.
The metal atom or ion around which the ligands are arranged in a coordination compound.
definitions.example: In the compound [Co(NH3)6]Cl3, Co3+ is the central metal ion.

definitions.definition: An ion or molecule that donates a pair of electrons to form a coordinate covalent bond with the central metal atom or ion.
definitions.example: NH3 and H2O are ligands in the compounds [Co(NH3)6]Cl3 and [Ni(H2O)6]SO4 respectively.

definitions.definition: The number of donor atoms of the ligands that are bonded to the central metal atom or ion in a coordination compound.
The central metal atom or ion with its attached ligands in a coordination compound.
definitions.example: [Co(NH3)6]3+ is the coordination entity in the compound [Co(NH3)6]Cl3.

definitions.definition: A coordination entity that carries a net charge.
A coordination entity that has no net charge.
The number of donor atoms provided by a single ligand to the central metal atom or ion in a coordination compound.
definitions.example: NH3 is a bidentate ligand with a denticity of 2.

definitions.definition: The occurrence of two or more compounds with the same molecular formula but different structures.
A type of isomerism that arises due to the differences in the spatial arrangement of the ligands in a coordination compound.
A type of isomerism that arises due to the presence of non-superimposable mirror images in a coordination compound.
A type of isomerism that arises due to the difference in the way a ligand is attached to the central metal atom or ion in a coordination compound.
A type of isomerism that arises due to the difference in the ions present in the coordination compound.
The central metal atom or ion along with its attached ligands in a coordination compound.
definitions.example: [Co(NH3)6]3+ is the coordination sphere in the compound [Co(NH3)6]Cl3.

definitions.definition: A theory that explains the magnetic and spectroscopic properties of coordination compounds based on the electrostatic field exerted by the ligands on the central metal atom or ion.
definitions.example: CFT is used to explain the magnetic properties of [Co(NH3)6]Cl3.

definitions.definition: The energy difference between the d-orbitals of the central metal atom or ion in the presence of an electrostatic field.
definitions.example: Δo is responsible for the splitting of d-orbitals in [Co(NH3)6]Cl3.
